admin-finder.trl_ceiling_reason: Authenticated admin discovery requires site-specific credentials or session state.
admin-finder.trl_evidence:
v1.6.0 — TRL 5→6 uplift.
- Bundled curated wordlist (~150 paths from SecLists + OWASP CMS + vendor docs)
  via go:embed; deduped, sorted, comment-stripped at parse time.
- Per-probe: HEAD then GET fallback (for 405/400 and body sniffing on 2xx);
  follows up to 2 manual redirects; per-request 3s, total 60s, worker pool 8.
- Login-form sniffer (password / username / sign in / log in / type=password)
  with strong-signal short-circuit ("password" alone) and 2-of-N rule.
- First-hop Location preserved across follow chain so "302 to /wp-admin/login"
  classifies as present even when the followed target is 404.
- Optional ?wordlist_url= composes with sibling go-pentest-wordlists
  (cap 500 entries, 256 KiB fetch budget, SSRF-safe via go-common/safehttp).
- SCOPE_GUARD_URL env (comma list, supports *.example.com) gates which
  hosts may be probed; off when unset.
- Backward-compat: ?target= alias for ?url=.
- Tests: handler_test.go + classify_test.go + wordlist_test.go;
  httptest fake server covers 200-with-form / 302-to-login / 401 /
  200-generic / 302-to-home / 404. `go test ./...` green.

analyze-headers.trl_evidence:
Full OWASP secure-header matrix: HSTS (max-age/includeSubDomains/preload),
CSP (parsed; flags unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval/wildcard/data:/http: in script-src
and style-src; checks default-src, object-src 'none', frame-ancestors), COOP,
COEP, CORP, Permissions-Policy (risky sinks parsed), Referrer-Policy,
X-Frame-Options (ALLOW-FROM deprecation), X-Content-Type-Options,
X-XSS-Protection (deprecated; flagged), Cache-Control, Server/X-Powered-By
information disclosure. SSRF-safe via go-common/safehttp; HEAD-first with
GET fallback when origin rejects HEAD. Composite grade is critical-aware:
any missing HSTS on HTTPS or missing CSP forces F. Split into headers.go,
csp.go, grade.go, handler.go; covered by handler_test.go (6 cases including
perfect-site, weak-CSP, missing-HSTS, deprecated-X-XSS) and csp_test.go (7
parser/grader cases). `go test ./...` green.

blocklist-checker.trl_evidence:
~30 curated DNSBL/RBL/URIBL zones across spam, phishing, malware,
tor, and open-proxy categories. Per-list 127.0.0.x return-code

SERVFAIL / "got 127.0.0.x". In-process miekg/dns test server with

broken-links.trl_evidence: 1.4.1: ErrorClass taxonomy with 14 stable kinds (timeout/dns_failure/conn_refused/conn_reset/tls_error/hard_404/hard_4xx/hard_5xx/unauthorized/rate_limited/soft_404/too_many_redirects/ssrf_blocked/unknown); Soft404Detector with per-origin baseline; redirect hop tracking; ClassCounts histogram; client.Fetch migration. 9/10 corpus PASS; verified evidence: 403 from twitter.com correctly classifies as 'unauthorized' not 'broken'.

bucket-finder.trl_ceiling_reason: Enumeration (proving bucket exists, listing contents) requires paid threat intel, leaked wordlists, or HackerOne project data.
bucket-finder.trl_evidence:
v1.4.0 adds permutation-based discovery (?name=acme): generates a
bounded candidate set (≤200) from base + corp/inc + role suffixes
(backup, prod, staging, assets, internal, …), probes every candidate
across AWS S3 (vhost + path), GCS (vhost + path), Azure Blob/File,
and DigitalOcean Spaces (5 regions) in parallel via a 16-worker pool
with a 4s per-probe and 90s total budget. Per-provider response
classification distinguishes listable / exists / denied / not_exists
(via NoSuchBucket vs AccessDenied / Azure AuthenticationFailed) and
attaches sample object keys + counts when a ListBucketResult or
EnumerationResults body is present. Optional WORDLISTS_URL extension
hook for go-pentest-wordlists. Test coverage: permute_test.go (8
cases), detect_test.go (12 cases), handler_test.go (httptest server
with canned XML returning listable/denied/NoSuchBucket/404). Backward
compatibility preserved for the legacy ?url= page-scan path. Pure Go,
CGO-free, SSRF-safe via go-common/safehttp. ADR:
docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

comment-extractor.trl_evidence: 1.3.1: classification across 11 CommentKind labels (boilerplate/ie_conditional/todo/deprecation/internal_path/internal_email/credential_leak/source_map/react_dev/vue_ssr/dev_note) with info/low/medium/high severity. Credential detectors: AWS access keys, GCP service tokens, GitHub tokens, Stripe live/test keys, JWTs, PEM private keys, inline api_key/password assignments. HTML entity decoding pre-classification. Summary.Notable surfaces medium+high findings. 22 unit tests. 10/10 corpus PASS.

company-size.trl_evidence:
TRL-6 uplift (2026-05-16): refactored from a generic size-tier estimator
into the canonical *classifier* sibling of go_team_size. Now produces
LinkedIn-style buckets (Self-employed / 1-10 / 11-50 / ... / 10001+),

pure POST {employees:N}, missing input, no-siblings local-only).
See docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

complete-gardener-planner.trl_evidence: planning app, calendar and checklist logic

compression-tester.trl_evidence:
Negotiation matrix probes identity + 4 algorithms (gzip/br/zstd/deflate)
with raw-bytes capture (Transport.DisableCompression=true), reports
compressed/decoded sizes, server preference (full Accept-Encoding), Vary
coverage, encoding-mismatch detection (decoder fails when server lies),
and CRIME/BREACH risk indicator. Pure-Go decoders (stdlib gzip/flate +
andybalholm/brotli + klauspost/compress/zstd; CGO-free). 8s per-probe / 30s
total budget. ≥6 unit tests cover gzip/brotli/identity round-trip, mismatch,
preference detection, and full handler shape via httptest.Server fixtures.

cookie-checker.trl_evidence: 1.3.1: publicsuffix-aware domain scope (etld1_scope/parent_scope/public_suffix_scope severity tiers replace single string-suffix heuristic); CMP+tracker cookie fingerprinting across 19 vendors (OneTrust, Cookiebot, Quantcast, etc. + GA/Facebook/Hotjar/CF); ClassCounts taxonomy {cmp,tracker,session,other}; secure_on_http warning. 10/10 corpus PASS; verified: __cf_bm correctly fingerprints to vendor=cloudflare.

cors-scanner.trl_evidence: Endpoint discovery, CORS preflight testing, credential+wildcard checks, risk categorization.

cors-tester.trl_evidence:
7-variant active prober (reflection, null, subdomain-suffix-bypass,
prefix-confusion, port-attack, case-fold, http-vs-https). Each variant
sends an OPTIONS preflight + GET, records the full ACAO/ACAC/Vary/
methods/headers surface, and feeds a documented severity matrix
(reflection x credentials, null x credentials, etc.). Worker pool of
4, per-probe 4s, total 30s. Composes with go-pentest-cors-misconfig-
prober via CORS_MISCONFIG_URL; gateable on bug-bounty scope via
SCOPE_GUARD_URL. Tests: probes_test (variant table, downgrade skip,
scheme preservation), evaluate_test (isReflection, isTrueACAC,
hasVaryOrigin, Classify matrix), handler_test (httptest servers for
reflective+credentials critical, wildcard-only low, suffix-bypass
critical, null-origin critical, safe server, case-fold, full JSON
shape) — 20+ test cases across 3 files. ADR docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-
to-6.md captures the variant taxonomy and severity matrix.

crawl-web-application.trl_evidence:
TRL 6 — BFS crawler with worker pool (default 8), per-host rate limit
(golang.org/x/time/rate, 4 rps), robots.txt parsing via temoto/robotstxt
(RFC 9309), HTML parsing via golang.org/x/net/html (extracts <a>, <link
rel=canonical>, title; honors rel=nofollow), URL canonicalization +
cross-page dedup, SSRF-safe outbound via go-common/safehttp, scope
enforcement (same-origin | same-domain | same-etld), bounded total scan
(90s) and partial-result return on deadline. Test coverage: ≥9 cases
including robots-respect, scope enforcement, max-pages cap, depth cap,
bad-URL handling, JSON shape — all green via httptest fake site.

crlf-tester.trl_evidence:
Marker-based detection (8-byte per-scan unique hex marker) replaces
static-string echo checks; inspect() classifies header reflection
(critical), raw-newline-in-header (high), and body reflection (medium)
separately. Probes inject across query params, path segments, and four
reflected headers (User-Agent, Referer, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Host;
Host gated by ALLOW_HOST_INJECT=1). Payload corpus is 20 variants
covering hex-case, double-encoding, Unicode/UTF-8 overlong, fragment-
prefixed, raw \r vs \n vs \r\n; corpus extensible via PAYLOADS_URL
(go-pentest-payloads). Per-request 4 s, total 60 s budget, worker pool
4. SCOPE_GUARD_URL fail-closed gate. ≥9 unit + integration tests
including httptest.Server vulnerable + safe backends.

crypto-finder.trl_evidence:
~30 vendor-specific regex rules (AWS/GitHub/Slack/Stripe/Google/Twilio/
SendGrid/Mailgun/Anthropic/OpenAI/HuggingFace/Discord/npm/JWT/PEM)
with severity classification, Shannon-entropy gating for the generic

Inline tests cover every vendor pattern + an entropy-edge negative.
ADR 0001 documents the rule taxonomy and fleet hookup.

currency-detector.trl_evidence: Symbol regex + locale patterns + currency-switcher heuristics.

customer-logos.trl_evidence:
TRL 6 (real). DOM-based extraction via golang.org/x/net/html (no regex
HTML parsing, no LLM, no image recognition). Multi-page probe over
/, /customers, /clients, /case-studies, /testimonials, /enterprise,
/partners with per-fetch 5s + total 20s wall-clock cap. Per-logo
confidence band derived from heading + grid + brand-source signals;
document verdict ladder (none → weak_signal → social_proof →
strong_social_proof). Brand normalisation strips logo/®/™ suffixes
and title-cases; dedup keys on dest_url host first, normalised brand
second. 19 unit tests covering extract/normalize/classify/handler
including an httptest.Server with a canned customers page (logo grid
+ "Trusted by" section + partners). See docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

d3-graph.trl_evidence: visualization library, mature D3 binding, test coverage expected

dir-listing.trl_evidence: RFC-compliant directory listing parser with SSRF guards, tests, and verdict structure

dns-record.trl_evidence:
Uses battle-tested github.com/miekg/dns (the canonical Go DNS lib);
parallel fan-out across A/AAAA/CNAME/MX/NS/TXT/SOA/CAA/SRV/PTR/
DNSKEY/DS/TLSA; multi-resolver round-robin (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, 9.9.9.9)
with explicit ?resolver= override; EDNS0 4096 buffer + automatic TCP
fallback on truncation; optional ?dnssec=1 chain check returning
secure|insecure|bogus|indeterminate against the parent zone's DS.
In-process miekg/dns server in tests (5 unit cases, no network) plus
one optional live integration test gated on DNS_LIVE=1. ADR
documenting choices at docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md. Hooks for
caa-checker (CAA RRset) and reverse-dns (PTR via in-addr.arpa).

domain-details.trl_evidence: RDAP-first via openrdap (IANA bootstrap, RFC 7483 structured fields incl. event actions, status codes, secureDNS); whois TCP-43 fallback via likexian/whois + whois-parser; per-source 5s / total 12s timeouts; 6h in-process cache; 8 unit tests over canned RDAP JSON + canned whois text fixtures (no network in CI); GDPR-aware contact handling; ADR docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

go-fleet-dns-sync.description: Hetzner DNS reconciler. Targets the Hetzner Cloud API (api.hetzner.cloud/v1, Bearer auth, RRSet-oriented) — the canonical DNS surface that supersedes the deprecated dns.hetzner.com Console API. Reads services-registry/services.json, diffs against actual zone state, applies the delta. Pulls the Cloud API token from go-fleet-secrets. 30-min ticker. Extras never auto-deleted.
go-fleet-dns-sync.trl_ceiling_reason: Per-zone instance; ceiling lifts with multi-zone via parallel deployments.
go-fleet-dns-sync.trl_evidence: Hetzner Cloud API client (api.hetzner.cloud/v1, Bearer auth) + RRSet-flattening on read + single-record RRSets on write + registry parser + 3-way diff + idempotent apply + paginated rrset listing. 13 tests cover diff/create/update/dry-run/extras-never-deleted/non-mesh-ignored.

go-fleet-graph.description: Fleet graph collector. Aggregates service-to-service call observations emitted by go-common/graph into 1m/1h/1d buckets in SQLite. Serves /events (ingest), /graph + /summary (queries), /service/<slug>/{callers,calls}, /lookup/<slug> (powers graph.Lookup), /drift (declared-vs-observed). Mirror of services-registry refreshes every 5min.
go-fleet-graph.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-node SQLite. Lifts to 8 once paired with periodic snapshot backup and a multi-collector consensus design for high-RPS callers.
go-fleet-graph.trl_evidence: Retention rollup goroutine (minute->hour->drop, env-tuned), drift webhook on new_edge or silent_dep>1h with 1h dedup, /selftest round-trip

go-fleet-preflight.description: Pre-deploy validation. POST /preflight {repo, secrets?} runs every required check in parallel: registry presence, DNS resolution, port collision, required secrets metadata. Returns 200 (all green) or 424 (any red) with detailed checklist. Read-only.
go-fleet-preflight.trl_ceiling_reason: Each new check is one goroutine. Lift bound is breadth of checks.
go-fleet-preflight.trl_evidence: check:image (crane HEAD ghcr.io), check:nginx (HTTPS+TLS expiry warn at 30d), 60s ETag-aware registry cache, /selftest against embedded service.yaml fixture with stubbed crane+DNS+HTTPS

go-fleet-secrets.description: Encrypted secrets vault. Holds infrastructure tokens (Hetzner DNS, GitHub PAT, SMTP, platform API keys). NaCl secretbox at rest, per-secret nonce, scoped reads via gateway-injected X-Auth-User against consumers allowlist, separate X-Admin-Token for writes. Audit log per access.
go-fleet-secrets.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-node SQLite. Ceiling lifts to 8 once paired with sealed offsite master-key backup.
go-fleet-secrets.trl_evidence: NaCl secretbox (XSalsa20-Poly1305) per-secret nonce; master key from env (fail-fast on missing); constant-time admin compare; metadata-only list; audit log every access. 16 tests pass.

go-fleet-visualizer.description: Fleet graph visualizer. Operator surface (keystore-auth) renders a force-directed live topology with click-to-drill, mesh filter, time-window selector, and a drift dialog comparing declared-vs-observed edges. Public surface at /public shows sanitised (caller -> target) counts only — no latencies, no internal hosts. Frontend uses vis-network from CDN, vanilla JS, no build step.
go-fleet-visualizer.trl_ceiling_reason: Vis-network handles ~500 nodes comfortably. Lifts to 7 with server-side layout caching + WebGL-backed renderer for >500-node fleets.
go-fleet-visualizer.trl_evidence: Two-surface auth split via selective middleware. Proxies to collector for all queries. Auto-refresh every 30s. SRI hash on CDN vis-network bundle. Path templating-aware drill-down. No tests yet — first iteration.

go-html-proxy.trl_evidence: Hexagonal/Clean Architecture in src/ (domain/application/infrastructure split), pipeline middleware with circuit-breakers, response cache + Prometheus metrics, k6 smoke/light/medium/load scripts, header-sanitizer + SSRF awareness on upstream fetch, fleet keystore auth via X-Auth-User header trust (synthetic fleet user, credit bypass) + local-user fallback via ?api_key=/X-API-Key/Authorization Bearer/?token=. Unit-tested auth chain incl. gateway-header + extract-token priority. Plus root-level fleet-canonical handler (ua_pool.go/retry.go/redirect.go/handler.go) at TRL 5 — UA rotation deterministic by X-Request-ID, retry on 5xx/network with 250ms backoff, configurable timeout/redirect cap, JSON envelope or raw passthrough, safehttp SSRF guard, 9 test cases (httptest server, 5xx-then-success, redirect chain). See docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-5.md.

Classifies real_severity end-to-end: critical / high / medium / low / informational. Renders a working PoC HTML page when chain elements present.
go-pentest-attack-chainer.trl_evidence: 4/4 unit tests passing (base-domain inference, severity matrix, PoC rendering with/without chain). Verified end-to-end against accommodations.booking.com: 30 siblings + 12 endpoint probes in 1.2s, correctly downgrades prober high -> informational.

go-pentest-audit-log.description: Append-only audit log for the pentest fleet. Every scan-start / scope-denied / finding-submitted gets recorded. Legal floor + CYA.
go-pentest-audit-log.trl_evidence: Pure-Go SQLite, deterministic (ts,actor,action,target,program,tool) hash for dedup, append-only by design.

go-pentest-bounty-scope-checker.description: Given a domain, returns which bug-bounty programs include it in scope (sorted by max payout). Embedded seed of ~10 well-known programs (GitHub, GitLab, Shopify, PayPal, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Tesla, Uber, X).
go-pentest-bounty-scope-checker.trl_ceiling_reason: Production freshness depends on continuously updated program feeds.
go-pentest-bounty-scope-checker.trl_evidence: Bug-bounty scope matcher uses curated program seed data, regex caching, payout sorting, structured matches, and tests.

go-pentest-cert-transparency.description: crt.sh wrapper with 24h SQLite cache. Returns SAN-derived subdomains or full cert records (issuer/dates/SANs).
go-pentest-cert-transparency.trl_ceiling_reason: Add Censys + Google CT in parallel for redundancy.
go-pentest-cert-transparency.trl_evidence: crt.sh fetcher + SQLite cache (modernc) + wildcard-skip + SAN-suffix filter; tested for normalization, cache hits, subdomain filter.

go-pentest-chaos-sync.description: Background syncer for ProjectDiscovery Chaos public dataset. SQLite-backed (modernc.org/sqlite, CGo-free) cache of subdomains keyed by root_domain; daily refresh; O(log N) lookups; zero target-network traffic.
go-pentest-chaos-sync.trl_ceiling_reason: Delta-tracking and program ranking needed for 7+.
go-pentest-chaos-sync.trl_evidence: v0.2.0 adds dataset_version (sha256(url|last_updated)) tracking, cursor-paginated /delta and /export endpoints, /stats with distinct-version counts, and admin-gated POST /sync; SQLite WAL + busy_timeout=5000 for syncer/reader concurrency; skip-unchanged short-circuit avoids redownloading unchanged Chaos zips; per-zip 60s HTTP timeout; ≥6 unit tests covering full sync, lookup, delta windowing, cursor pagination correctness across ≥3 pages, dataset_version stability, and admin-token gating; httptest fixtures serve canned chaos index + canned subdomain zips so tests are CGO-free and offline.

go-pentest-codec.description: Encode/decode + JWT segment decoder. 11 formats (base64 std/url/raw, base32, hex, url, html, gzip, zlib, json-string); alg=none/path-traversable-kid flags on JWT.
go-pentest-codec.trl_evidence: Pure-stdlib codec table; full roundtrip tests; JWT decoder flags dangerous algs.

go-pentest-continuous-monitor.description: Daily cron-style scheduler that diffs asset-inventory per program and enqueues scan jobs only for NEW assets. Turns one-shot recon into a subscription — money compounds with the asset universe, not manpower.
go-pentest-continuous-monitor.trl_ceiling_reason: Bounded by job-queue throughput. Sequential per-program scheduler keeps the reasoning simple.
go-pentest-continuous-monitor.trl_evidence: Concurrent dispatch bounded by errgroup+semaphore, per-program advisory lock via running_until (RFC3339), deterministic idempotency_key sent to job-queue (composes with job-queue 0.2.0), /selftest

go-pentest-cors-misconfig-prober.description: Active CORS-misconfig prober. 5 attacker-Origin probes (reflection, null-origin, suffix-bypass, pre-subdomain, case-fold). Reflection+Allow-Credentials=high.
go-pentest-cors-misconfig-prober.trl_evidence: 5 active probes, reflection+credentials high-sev detection, suffix-confusion + pre-subdomain bypass, case-fold. Tested positive (high) + negative.

go-pentest-cvss.description: CVSS 3.1 base score calculator. Pure-Go FIRST.org spec implementation. Takes a vector, returns base/severity/impact/exploitability.
go-pentest-cvss.trl_evidence: Pure-Go CVSS 3.1; tested vs 6 canonical FIRST.org examples incl. 0.0/10.0 boundaries and scope-changed XSS. Stateless; CPU-only.

go-pentest-dashboard.description: Browser dashboard for the pentest fleet. Single-page HTML app (Tailwind CDN, vanilla JS) with tabs for Preflight, Programs, Recon, Scan, Findings, Report. Calls every passive scanner via the api_key in browser localStorage. Human-facing GUI for the same flow go-pentest-cli and go-pentest-walkthrough automate.
go-pentest-dashboard.trl_ceiling_reason: Bounded by what the fleet exposes; active probing tools need auth + deploy before they can be surfaced safely.
go-pentest-dashboard.trl_evidence: Single index.html, no build step, CORS-permissive fleet endpoints. 6 tabs covering recon → scan → triage → render.

go-pentest-dedup-fingerprint.description: Semantic dedup of findings. Normalizes query-param order, UUIDs in path, timestamps, whitespace in body, then sha256s. Stops one XSS reported by 4 scanners from looking like 4 dupes.
go-pentest-dedup-fingerprint.trl_ceiling_reason: Normalization rule corpus grows with bug-class variety; current 6 rules handle the common cases.
go-pentest-dedup-fingerprint.trl_evidence: 73-row scanner corpus across 4 scanners x 8 vulns: intra=100% / inter=0% collision rates; fp_version=v1; /forget admin-gated; /selftest

go-pentest-dependency-cve.description: CPE -> NVD JSON 2.0 + CISA KEV cross-flag. Per-request fetch (no local cache yet). Composes with go-pentest-httpx tech detection.
go-pentest-dependency-cve.trl_ceiling_reason: TRL 7 with local CVE feed primitive caching NVD/GHSA delta-updates.
go-pentest-dependency-cve.trl_evidence: SQLite TTL cache (NVD/KEV/OSV), OSV.dev cross-source merge, /selftest with stubbed upstreams, 14 test funcs (was 0)

go-pentest-disclosure-policy-finder.description: Probes 12 disclosure-policy paths and classifies bounty/VDP/safe-harbor/unknown/none. Extracts Contact/PGP from security.txt and detects platform hints (HackerOne/Bugcrowd/Intigriti/YesWeHack/Synack).
go-pentest-disclosure-policy-finder.trl_evidence: 12 probe paths, RFC 9116 parser, 9 bounty-platform regexes, 4 safe-harbor regexes, class-priority resolver. Extends fleet security-txt with VDP-vs-bounty classification.

go-pentest-exploit-verifier.description: Gate between possible-finding and submit-bot. Runs deterministic confirmation per vuln class: SSRF via OOB callback, XSS via marker reflection, IDOR via session-stripped re-fetch, JWT alg-none, open-redirect, subdomain-takeover.
go-pentest-exploit-verifier.trl_ceiling_reason: Each verifier is a thin recipe — lifts as verifier coverage grows. Bounded by OOB collector quality for blind classes.
go-pentest-exploit-verifier.trl_evidence: TRL-5 uplift 2026-05-16: introduced explicit confirmation taxonomy (verdict: confirmed|not_confirmed|inconclusive) downstream services gate on; per-verifier 10s + total 30s budgets; SSRF verifier emits TRL-5 evidence shape (callback_recorded, callback_at, raw_request) and polls oob-collector up to 5s; XSS marker matcher accepts raw + HTML-entity + URL-escaped echoes; JWT-alg-none, open-redirect, IDOR, takeover all set explicit verdicts; scope-guard fail-closed yields verdict:inconclusive (never confirmed). SQLite migration adds verdict column + idx_ver_verdict, backfills from confirmed. Tests: 12 stubbed-dependency cases incl. verdict-classification, per-verifier timeout, malformed-JWT inconclusive, encoded-XSS marker reflection. ADR docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-5.md captures composition decisions. Real fleet integration (oob-collector + takeover-fingerprints + findings-store wired end-to-end) still pending.

go-pentest-favicon-hash.description: Shodan-style mmh3 favicon hash with known-tech lookup (~15 seeds: Tomcat, Spring Boot, Jenkins, Grafana, Jupyter, phpMyAdmin, GitLab, FortiNet, F5, etc.).
go-pentest-favicon-hash.trl_evidence: mmh3 via spaolacci/murmur3, Python-compatible base64.encodebytes (76-char wrap), embedded lookup. Tests on determinism + sensitivity.

go-pentest-finding-triage.description: Auto-grades raw findings into submit_ready / needs_review / drop using 8 rules (missing fields, fingerprint-dupe, scope-deny, noise-class, confidence floor, severity promote, evidence-present, verified-class). Reasons array surfaces every decision.
go-pentest-finding-triage.trl_ceiling_reason: Lift bound is rule-corpus size. Adding ML or LLM-based triage would push the ceiling but break the pure-rule explainability.
go-pentest-finding-triage.trl_evidence: SQLite decision ledger with /decisions query, dry_run mode returning would_decide, sony/gobreaker circuit breakers around scope-guard + dedup-fingerprint, degraded_mode in response, /selftest

go-pentest-findings-store.description: Central deduplicated findings store for the pentest fleet. SQLite-backed (pure Go modernc); collapses same finding from multiple scanners via dedup_key with seen_count.
go-pentest-findings-store.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-node SQLite is fine until ~1M findings; horizontal shard or Postgres later.
go-pentest-findings-store.trl_evidence: findings_history shadow table with SHA-256 evidence hashing, /findings/{id}/history endpoint, /selftest round-trip

go-pentest-github-dorks.description: GitHub Search API dorking for leaked credentials: 31 curated patterns (AWS keys, .env, private keys, Slack/Stripe/PAT tokens, kubeconfig, npm/Docker creds). Rate-limit aware; pairs with go-pentest-trufflehog for verification.
go-pentest-github-dorks.trl_ceiling_reason: GitHub Search has no regex; semantic dedup + verifier-loop integration cap the climb at 6.
go-pentest-github-dorks.trl_evidence: 38 curated dork patterns (creds + files + vendors + infra + k8s + per-vendor leaks); typed rate-limit handling (primary + secondary/abuse) with adaptive sleep; per-dork 4s + total 90s deadline; optional verification compose with go-pentest-trufflehog (TRUFFLEHOG_URL); domain qualifier (\"<domain>\" in:file); production mode (MODE=production) refuses to start without GITHUB_TOKEN; 19 unit tests including httptest-mocked GitHub API (success / primary rate-limit / abuse / no-results) + trufflehog compose. Pure Go (CGO-free), no parallelism. Ceiling stays at 7 pending cross-dork dedup, snippet-level secret extraction, and findings-store fan-out.

go-pentest-http-replay.description: Captures + reproduces HTTP request/response pairs as bug-bounty evidence. Generates canonical curl PoC, replays for diff, imports raw HTTP from Burp. Uses go-common safehttp (SSRF-safe).
go-pentest-http-replay.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-node SQLite. Fine for evidence retention at typical bug-bounty scale; lifts when paired with object storage for large bodies.
go-pentest-http-replay.trl_evidence: go-common safehttp client; 256KB response cap; sha256 body-hash for rerun diff; 19 tests pass including curl-builder edge cases.

go-pentest-httpx.description: One HTTP fingerprint per request: status, title, server, TLS cert summary, ~25 tech-detection signatures. Parallel batch with worker cap. Replaces chaining tech-stack + security-headers + cdn-detector.
go-pentest-httpx.trl_ceiling_reason: Wrap wappalyzergo for full 3000+ fingerprint DB.
go-pentest-httpx.trl_evidence: Single+batch probes, parallel with worker cap, TLS extract, ~25 tech-detection signatures (CDNs, frameworks, CMS, front-end libs). safehttp-protected. Tested.

go-pentest-job-queue.description: Durable workflow queue. POST jobs, workers /claim → /complete or /fail. Crash-resilient via SQLite WAL; expired leases auto-requeue every 15s with exponential backoff. Backbone for orchestrator + continuous-monitor + every long-running scan.
go-pentest-job-queue.trl_ceiling_reason: Single-node SQLite. Fine up to ~10k jobs/sec; graduates to NATS JetStream or Postgres skip-locked beyond that.
go-pentest-job-queue.trl_evidence: idempotency_key column with partial UNIQUE index, /metrics short-circuit middleware, /selftest, TestEnqueue_IdempotencyKey_Concurrent (50-goroutine race)

go-pentest-leak-bounty-policy.description: Curated registry: which bug-bounty programs PAY for credential-leak disclosures, broken down by leak class (own_employee / shared_secret / customer_cred / canary). Use BEFORE writing up a leaked-credential disclosure — most platforms treat customer-side leaks as goodwill-only; only Stripe / GitHub partner / AWS canary / GitLab pay per-leak. 25 programs seeded.
go-pentest-leak-bounty-policy.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling 7 with quarterly auto-refresh from each program's policy URL + admin /reload endpoint. Pure manual curation caps at 5 because policies drift faster than humans can re-audit at scale.
go-pentest-leak-bounty-policy.trl_evidence: 25 hand-curated programs with per-program LeakPolicy across 4 leak classes (own_employee/shared_secret/customer_cred/canary) and credential_prefixes for token classification. 7 unit tests pin: registry parses, slugs unique, prefixes unique, sk_live_ -> Stripe + canary in_scope, shpat_ -> Shopify + customer_cred out_of_scope, >=3 canary-paying programs.

go-pentest-notify.description: Multi-sink notification fanout for the pentest fleet (Telegram/Discord/Slack/email/webhook/ntfy/Matrix/Teams/etc.) via containrrr/shoutrrr.
go-pentest-notify.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling = shoutrrr sink count + HMAC webhook auth.
go-pentest-notify.trl_evidence: Wraps containrrr/shoutrrr v0.8 (20+ sinks). Default-from-env, per-request URL override, secret-hiding /sinks.

go-pentest-nuclei.description: Nuclei wrapper: scope-guarded YAML-template scanner (cves/exposures/misconfigs/takeovers). Pre-flights every target through go-pentest-scope-guard; forwards findings to go-pentest-findings-store. Bundles nuclei binary in container.
go-pentest-nuclei.trl_ceiling_reason: Pinned-binary wrapping; templates refresh cadence + concurrency stress-testing gate the climb to 7.
go-pentest-nuclei.trl_evidence: Wraps nuclei binary; scope-guard pre-flight (fail-closed); -jsonl streaming parser; best-effort findings forwarding via safehttp; 5 unit tests covering 400/405/scope-empty/templates/JSONL-parse.

go-pentest-oob-collector.description: Out-of-band HTTP callback catcher for blind XSS/SSRF/RCE findings. Allocates short-lived tokens, public /c/{token} catch endpoint, authenticated polling API. v0.2 wraps projectdiscovery/interactsh for DNS exfil.
go-pentest-oob-collector.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling unlocked once projectdiscovery/interactsh is wrapped for DNS+SMTP exfil. HTTP half remains useful as simple-case catcher.
go-pentest-oob-collector.trl_evidence: v0.2 uplift: UUID-v4 token allocation (POST /alloc) with TTL clamp (60s..7d), public catch endpoint (/c/<token>) with 10KiB body cap + Referer capture, cursor-paginated poll (GET /poll/<token>?since=N), SSE long-poll (/poll/<token>/stream) with replay-on-reconnect, admin-gated revoke (DELETE /token/<token>), aggregate /stats. Per-token poll bucket (1 rps, burst 2). SQLite WAL mode for concurrent writer (catch) + reader (poll). 20 tests across alloc / catch / poll / stream / revoke / stats / body-cap / expired-token / rate-limit / cursor-delta — race-clean. Designed-for-fleet hookups (xss-scanner / file-inclusion canary / crlf-tester / findings-store) documented in docs/adr/0001.

go-pentest-orchestrator.description: Master end-to-end pipeline. POST a campaign and the state machine drives scope→recon→scan→triage→verify→submit through every other go-pentest-* service. 30s ticker auto-advances. SUBMIT_DRY_RUN=1 by default — the unattended money loop.
go-pentest-orchestrator.trl_ceiling_reason: Bounded by submit-bot's platform-API stability. Once submit-bot ships TRL 8, this trails one step behind at 7-8.
go-pentest-orchestrator.trl_evidence: SQLite state machine; idempotent per-state advance; append-only event log; 30s ticker; 16 tests pass with every fleet dep stubbed (httptest mux).

go-pentest-payloads.description: Serves payload corpora (XSS/SQLi/SSRF/SSTI/XXE/CRLF/NoSQLi/GraphQL/path-traversal) by class+variant. Random-sample API for taint fuzzing.
go-pentest-payloads.trl_evidence: 9 classes seeded with real-world payloads (xss reflected+polyglot, sqli error+time, ssrf cloud-meta+local, ssti jinja2+twig, xxe, crlf, nosqli, graphql, path-traversal). Random + filter APIs tested.

go-pentest-payoff-tracker.description: Self-evaluator. POST /predict captures what finding-triage said before submission; POST /outcome captures what the platform did with it (via triager-listener). GET /calibration returns per-rule precision + per-program ROI. Closes the learning loop.
go-pentest-payoff-tracker.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling lifts once at least ~20 real submissions have flowed through and /calibration is non-empty.
go-pentest-payoff-tracker.trl_evidence: Pure-Go SQLite, upsert prediction + outcome keyed on finding_id, LEFT JOIN-backed findings list, per-rule precision (paid / (paid+dropped)), per-program $/paid + hit-rate. Sync endpoint pulls watches from triager-listener. Unit tests cover join semantics + multi-rule firing + program-level ROI math.

go-pentest-rate-coordinator.description: Global per-host token-bucket rate limiter. Every active prober calls POST /acquire {host, weight} before firing — prevents multiple scanners from collectively DDoS-ing a target.
go-pentest-rate-coordinator.trl_ceiling_reason: In-process state — single instance. Multi-instance needs Redis (sethvargo/go-limiter has the backend ready).
go-pentest-rate-coordinator.trl_evidence: Prometheus /metrics (counter+histogram+gauges), /selftest spawns 100 concurrent acquirers, TestAcquire_Concurrent under -race; 0.2.1 fixes /metrics double-registration panic

go-pentest-report-templater.description: Turns a structured Finding into a platform-ready markdown report. Ships HackerOne/Bugcrowd/Intigriti templates. Optional CVSS enrichment via go-pentest-cvss. Highest-leverage service in the fleet.
go-pentest-report-templater.trl_ceiling_reason: Add org-specific templates + screenshot embed + direct HackerOne API submission.
go-pentest-report-templater.trl_evidence: 3 platform templates using stdlib text/template, custom func map (title/code/indent/add1), CVSS enrichment via go-pentest-cvss with graceful degrade. Tested for render + missing-template + per-platform output.

go-pentest-scope-guard.description: Hard scope check before any active prober fires. POST /check {target, program_id} -> {allowed, reason, matched_by}. Matches hostname/wildcard/CIDR/regex; exclusions always win. Legal floor of the pentest fleet.
go-pentest-scope-guard.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling is whatever feeds it programs — once go-pentest-bounty-scope-checker exists, scope-guard is a CDN over its data.
go-pentest-scope-guard.trl_evidence: SQLite persistence with 256-LRU, audit_log with /audit query, ReDoS pattern rejection at create-time, target-normalizer composition with 3-strike circuit breaker, /selftest

go-pentest-screenshot.description: Headless-Chromium screenshot evidence for pentest reports. POST /shot {url,viewport,before_js,after_js} → PNG + DOM hash + title + status. 24h SQLite cache keyed by sha256 of inputs.
go-pentest-screenshot.trl_ceiling_reason: Bounded by Chromium-headless RAM (1 GiB shm) and the fact that one container is one Chromium instance.
go-pentest-screenshot.trl_evidence: chromedp + Chromium in Alpine container; pure-Go SQLite cache; 14 tests pass via fake Capturer (no Chromium needed to build).

go-pentest-secrets-scanner.description: Regex+entropy secret scanner. ~30 vendor rules (AWS, GitHub, GitLab, Slack, Stripe, Google, Twilio, SendGrid, Mailgun, Anthropic, OpenAI, HF, Discord, npm, JWT, RSA/EC/OpenSSH/PGP keys).
go-pentest-secrets-scanner.trl_ceiling_reason: TRL 8 with verified-secret feature (call vendor API to confirm key is live).
go-pentest-secrets-scanner.trl_evidence: ~30 vendor rules, Shannon-entropy gate via math.Log2, line+context+redaction. Tested vs real-looking fixtures.

go-pentest-subfinder.description: Multi-source passive subdomain enumeration: crt.sh (via our own cert-transparency), HackerTarget, AnubisDB, Wayback. Free sources only.
go-pentest-subfinder.trl_evidence: 4 passive sources fanned out in parallel with per-source timing + error capture, merged with dedup + suffix filter. No paid keys required.

go-pentest-submit-bot.description: Files findings to HackerOne / Bugcrowd / Intigriti via their APIs, or via security.txt email as fallback. Rate-limit + daily-cap aware. Dry-run mode for verification. The actual money step.
go-pentest-submit-bot.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling lifts to 8 once real H1/BC/Intigriti creds are verified end-to-end and the rendered-markdown shape is confirmed against the platforms' parsers.
go-pentest-submit-bot.trl_evidence: 4 platform adapters (H1/BC/Intigriti/email) with stubbed-creds + rate-limit + daily-cap. 23 tests pass via httptest. NOT yet round-tripped against real platform APIs — TRL stays at 4 until verified end-to-end.

go-pentest-takeover-checker.description: Subdomain-takeover scanner. Resolves CNAME, probes HTTP, matches signatures from go-pentest-takeover-fingerprints. Severity none/possible/likely.
go-pentest-takeover-checker.trl_evidence: 2-of-3 multi-resolver quorum, confirmed-tier registration probes (GitHub/S3/Heroku), findings-store wire with fail-open, 20 tests across 4 packages

go-pentest-takeover-fingerprints.description: Subdomain takeover signature DB. 17 curated vendors (AWS S3, GitHub Pages, Heroku, Fastly, Shopify, Vercel, Netlify, Azure, Cargo, WordPress, Tumblr, Zendesk, Surge, Ghost, ReadMe, Intercom, Unbounce).
go-pentest-takeover-fingerprints.trl_evidence: 17 curated signatures from can-i-take-over-xyz + in-the-wild. CNAME+status+body multi-signal match. Tested positive (S3) + negative cases.

go-pentest-target-normalizer.description: Canonicalizes free-form pentest targets (URL/host/IP/CIDR). Returns kind, eTLD+1, private-IP flag. Foundation primitive every active prober calls before doing anything else.
go-pentest-target-normalizer.trl_ceiling_reason: Ceiling is whatever publicsuffix list covers; no external deps to break.
go-pentest-target-normalizer.trl_evidence: 55-row //go:embed golden corpus, /selftest with idempotency assertion, NormalizedVia evidence trail, psl_version in /version

go-pentest-triager-listener.description: Inbound half of submit-bot. POST /watch registers a submitted bounty report; a 15-min ticker polls HackerOne / Bugcrowd / Intigriti and records state changes (new -> triaged -> duplicate/informative/n_a/resolved). Without it the fleet cannot learn its own duplicate rate or payoff per program.
go-pentest-triager-listener.trl_ceiling_reason: Bounded by platform API stability — H1/BC/Intigriti reshape attributes regularly and there's no signed contract.
go-pentest-triager-listener.trl_evidence: SQLite-backed, idempotent on (platform, external_id), terminal-state poll skipping, state-change journal, normalized status enum across 3 platform shapes. Adapter stubs for H1/BC/Intigriti return ErrNotConfigured cleanly when tokens absent.

go-pentest-trufflehog.description: Trufflehog wrapper: scans repos/git/filesystem for VERIFIED secrets (--only-verified). 700+ detectors live-tested against providers. Upgrades go-pentest-secrets-scanner with verification.
go-pentest-trufflehog.trl_ceiling_reason: Wraps upstream trufflehog binary; advancing requires custom concurrency control and deduplication.
go-pentest-trufflehog.trl_evidence: wraps pinned trufflehog binary (v3.83.0) with cmdRunner injection seam → ≥13 unit tests covering verified hits, mixed verified/unverified, scope-deny + fail-open, scan timeout, multi-detector summary, async cutover, concurrency cap. Per-finding output carries detector, source(file:line), commit, redacted (first/last 4), raw_match_hash (sha256), severity. Composes with go-pentest-scope-guard (POST /check, fail-open with note), go-pentest-findings-store (POST /findings, dedup_key includes fingerprint), go-pentest-job-queue (POST /jobs at >30s estimated runtime). Hard caps: 4 concurrent scans, 5min wall-clock per scan, 1MiB request body, argv-injection-safe flag value sanitisation.

go-pentest-walkthrough.description: HTTP equivalent of go-pentest-cli — single-domain pentest flow as REST API. /preflight, /recon, /findings, /report + /sessions for async scan with per-finding manual triage override. Pause/resume per session.
go-pentest-walkthrough.trl_ceiling_reason: Same ceiling as orchestrator — bounded by submit-bot end-to-end verification.
go-pentest-walkthrough.trl_evidence: Same idempotent state-machine shape as orchestrator + manual-override hooks (decide, pause, advance). 22 tests pass over stub fleet endpoints.

go-pentest-wayback-urls.description: Historical URL discovery via web.archive.org CDX. Returns every URL ever crawled with status + MIME + auto-flagged interesting extensions (.bak/.sql/.env/.zip).
go-pentest-wayback-urls.trl_evidence: Wayback CDX API client; MIME+status filters; ~15 interesting-extension signatures (backup/config/key/sqlite/dump).

go-pentest-wordlists.description: Serves curated pentest wordlists (dirs, subdomains, params, fuzz) by category with SHA256 integrity. Random-sample API. Designed to grow via SecLists submodule.
go-pentest-wordlists.trl_evidence: Disk-backed catalog with SHA256, reservoir-sample random API, paged reads. Ships with starter dirs/subdomains/params. CPU-only.

google-taxonomy.trl_evidence:
Embedded Google Product Taxonomy snapshot (5595 nodes, version 2021-09-21,
taxonomy-with-ids.en-US.txt). Three documented endpoints: /lookup (id→path),
/search (fuzzy via lithammer/fuzzysearch RankFindNormalizedFold), /classify
(TF-IDF cosine over leaf+ancestor terms, ancestor weight 0.5, leaf weight 2.0,
smoothed IDF). Inverted index built once at startup, vectors precomputed.
9 unit tests cover parser depth/parent, search ranking, search top-K cap,
classify ranking, classify matched_terms, GET/POST/JSON-body shapes, lookup
hit/miss/bad-input, legacy backwards-compat envelope. CGO-free, no runtime
network calls. Ceiling: TF-IDF without stemming/synonyms misses "shirt"↔
"shirts" — TRL 6 would need a stemmer + synonym map.

graphql-introspection.trl_ceiling_reason: Authenticated GraphQL schemas need user-supplied tokens and role-aware probing.
graphql-introspection.trl_evidence:
TRL 6 (real). Beyond endpoint discovery + introspection summary,
v3 adds three orthogonal abuse probes used by every commercial GraphQL
scanner (Inspectiv, GraphCrawler, InQL): alias-batching DoS multiplier,
field-suggestion info-leak, and text/plain CSRF bypass. Findings are
emitted with stable severity-tagged classes (introspection-enabled,
alias-batching-unbounded, csrf-possible, field-suggestions-enabled,
introspection-partial) so the catalog UI and sibling services
(api-extractor, swagger-finder) join on `class` strings without any
per-service mapping table. 21 Go test functions covering ≥6 server-
behaviour fixtures (canned introspection, explicit-disabled,
field-suggestion typo, alias-batched echo, CSRF accept, CSRF block).
See docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

hidden-fields.trl_evidence:
Pure-Go x/net/html DOM walker. Per-field role taxonomy (csrf-token,
honeypot, state, id, timestamp, flag, viewstate, session, other) plus
six risk rules (csrf-on-get, honeypot-default, sensitive-plaintext,
state-without-nonce, timestamp-future/epoch-zero, javascript-action).
Token-like values masked before serialisation (raw value never escapes
the process). 23 unit + integration tests against canned login,
signup, OAuth, GET-csrf, javascript:-action, and PII fixtures via
httptest.Server. safehttp SSRF guard + 5s per-request / 10s total
budget. ADR docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md.

hsts-checker.trl_ceiling_reason: needs real browser DOM for verification
hsts-checker.trl_evidence:
Pure-Go RFC 6797 directive parser with case-insensitive directive names,
quoted-string handling, negative/garbage rejection, and whitespace
tolerance (parse_test.go covers 9 cases). Embedded Chromium HSTS preload
list snapshot revision 2026-05-16.1 (~190 entries) with TLD-wide
treatment for .gov/.dev/.app/.page/.new and parent-domain inheritance.
Plaintext HTTP→HTTPS redirect probe captures the full chain (up to 5
hops) and reports the final scheme. Standalone TLS handshake check
records negotiated version, cipher suite, full chain length, and
performs explicit hostname + chain verification against the system
trust store. Grading rubric (F/D/C/B/A/A+) per
docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-6.md, with A+ requiring corroboration from
the bundled snapshot (not just the preload directive on the wire).
Test surface: parse_test.go + preload_test.go + grade_test.go +
handler_test.go (httptest.NewTLSServer for HSTS variants, separate
http test server for the redirect probe).

http-method-tester.trl_evidence:
Multi-stage probe (OPTIONS preflight → 15-verb sweep → method-override
probe) with structured findings for verb tampering, TRACE/XST, and
honored override headers. SSRF-guarded transport via
go-common/safehttp. Per-request 4s and total 30s deadlines, parallel
fan-out capped at 4. Code split into methods.go / probe.go /
detect.go / handler.go. Fixture-based unit tests
(probe_test.go + handler_test.go, ≥4 cases: clean GET-only origin,
permissive PUT, TRACE echo XST, anything-goes verb tampering, plus
legacy-shape and context-cancel checks). Backwards-compat 1.2.0 wire
shape preserved (`tool`, `target`, `methods_allowed`, `total_found`).
Detection rationale and consumer hookup documented in
docs/adr/0001-trl-uplift-to-5.md.

http-protocol.trl_evidence:
Five-protocol probe matrix (HTTP/0.9, /1.0, /1.1, /2, /3) implemented
with per-protocol 5s budget and 30s overall cap. HTTP/0.9 sends a
versionless "GET /\r\n" over plain TCP; /1.0 + /1.1 use hand-crafted
request lines over raw TCP/TLS; /2 uses golang.org/x/net/http2 with
ALPN h2; /3 uses github.com/quic-go/quic-go (pure Go, CGO-free).
Outputs a structured JSON envelope with per-probe outcome
(ok/refused/timeout/protocol-error/not_attempted), status, alpn
offered/negotiated, server header, time_ms, plus alt_svc parsing
(RFC 7838) for h3 advertisements and a summary {highest_supported,
supports_h2, supports_h3}. Test suite: handler_test.go +
protocol_test.go cover SSRF refusal, ?url= alias, h1.1 against
httptest.Server, h2 against httptest.NewTLSServer with ALPN h2,
h3 timeout against a closed UDP port, alt-svc parser table, and
HTTP/0.9 protocol-error detection against a synthetic HTTP/1.x
responder. SSRF guard via go-common safehttp; private ranges only
reachable with ALLOW_PRIVATE=1 (test-only).

idor-finder.trl_ceiling_reason: True IDOR proof requires authenticated sessions and object ownership context.
idor-finder.trl_evidence:
TRL 6 (2026-05-16). Detects four ID shapes (integer, UUID,
MongoDB ObjectId, base64); per-shape variant strategies (sequential
±N, UUID segment rotation, ObjectId counter flip, base64 last-byte
flip); auth-strip + auth-swap probes promote auth-bypass to a
separate severity track; user-data sniffer (email, name, role,
phone, SSN, credit card, address, token) drives the high-vs-medium
severity split. Verdict aggregator collapses probe-level signals
into one of {clean, suspicious, likely_idor, critical, inconclusive}.
Scope-guard via SCOPE_GUARD_URL; second-opinion via VERIFIER_URL →
go-pentest-exploit-verifier. 32-test suite covers ID detection,
variant generators, classifier, plus end-to-end fixtures (vulnerable,
auth-guarded, 404, generic-page).

iframe-analyzer.trl_evidence: 1.3.1: real CSP3 §6.7.7.7 frame-ancestors source-list parser (none/self/wildcard/scheme/host with port/path/wildcard kinds); X-Frame-Options parser (DENY/SAMEORIGIN/ALLOW-FROM with deprecation flag); ComputeFramingPosture cross-check returning {deny,sameorigin,explicit,open}+Conflicts; client.Fetch. 10/10 corpus PASS; verified: Cloudflare correctly flagged with verdict=deny + conflict 'CSP says deny, XFO disagrees: sameorigin'.

ipv6-checker.trl_evidence:
v2.0.0 uplift from TRL 3 to TRL 6:
- Per-record A/AAAA resolution via miekg/dns with explicit TTL +
  source-resolver attribution (1.1.1.1 / 8.8.8.8 fallback).
- Family-pinned TCP probes (tcp4/tcp6) on ports 80 + 443 with a
  3s connect timeout each; reports open/closed/timeout/skipped.
- Real TLS handshake on reachable 443 with strict SNI (no
  InsecureSkipVerify), reports offered + negotiated ALPN.
- Dual-stack classification (ipv4-only / ipv6-only / dual-stack /
  no-records).
- Per-family connect_ms recorded so consumers can compute Happy
  Eyeballs (RFC 8305) fallback cost.
- SSRF guard refuses private/loopback/link-local/CGNAT addresses
  unless ALLOW_PRIVATE=1.
- Resolver + dialer interfaces injected from tests; 4-case
  classification matrix + probe + happy-eyeballs unit tests pass.
- 15s total request budget enforced via context.WithTimeout.

jsonp-finder.trl_evidence:

Probes use a per-request marker callback (`marker_<random>`) so JSONP
detection is not fooled by partial reflections of a fixed name. Severity
classification is centralised, table-driven, and pinned by tests; auth-
shaped data shapes (email/session/token/userid) escalate to critical,
PII to high, generic public to medium/info, with a documented one-band
downgrade when X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is present and the
content-type is not a JS variant. Callback-XSS is confirmed via a
reflection probe (`<script>alert(1)</script>`). 14 unit tests + 5
httptest scenarios (auth-data JSONP, public-list JSONP w/ nosniff+CORS,
callback-XSS-vulnerable, plain JSON non-JSONP, summary roll-up). Total
cap 30s, per-request 4s, SSRF guard via go-common/safehttp.

team-size.trl_evidence:
TRL 6 — multi-signal team-size estimator with composite confidence.
Direct claims (regex on "team of N people", "N+ employees", "founded
with N"), schema.org Organization.numberOfEmployees (number, string,
QuantitativeValue, min/max range), LinkedIn-style bucket badges
(11-50 / 51-200 / 201-500 / 501-1000 / 1001-5000 / 5001-10000 /
10000+), DOM team-card / people-list counts, /careers open-job
counts. Composite estimator ranks signals by confidence, cross-
validates within ±20% (each agreement +0.04 conf, capped 0.97),
emits LinkedIn bucket label for sibling biz-classifier / company-
size services. ADR 0001 documents the weighting decisions. ≥12 unit
tests across claims/estimate/handler, all green.

telnet-banner.trl_evidence:
- RFC 854 IAC parser: handles 3-byte commands, IAC SB ... IAC SE blocks,
  escaped 0xFF, and truncated trailing bytes (telnet_test.go covers all four).
- Fingerprint table: Cisco IOS / IOS-XR, Juniper JUNOS, MikroTik RouterOS,
  Microsoft Telnet, HP iLO, Dell DRAC, QNAP, Synology, Linux getty —
  rule-ordered with version regexes (fingerprint_test.go covers 7 cases).
- End-to-end handler tests spin up an in-process TCP listener that emits
  canned banners (Cisco, Linux getty, Microsoft Telnet w/ IAC negotiation,
  raw-mode passthrough) — handler_test.go, 8 cases.
- Scope guard: refuses RFC1918 / loopback / link-local / IPv6 ULA targets
  unless ALLOW_PRIVATE=1; documented in ADR 0001.
- Code split into dial.go / telnet.go / fingerprint.go / handler.go for
  auditable per-concern review.

tos-finder.trl_evidence: Curated 30+ canonical paths for ToS/Privacy/DMCA/etc + freshness check.

trust-decay.trl_evidence:
v2.1.0 — Composite trust-decay score 0..100 from CPU-only weighted signals
(TLS cert age + chain trust + wildcard SNI, HSTS-vs-cert lifetime mismatch,
sitemap.xml lastmod freshness, robots.txt + security.txt age, copyright year
regex, HTML Last-Modified header age, deprecated <meta http-equiv> charset
hint, mixed-content references on https pages with -15 cap, outdated jQuery /
Bootstrap / AngularJS via <script src> regex, and dead-link probe over the
first 5 in-page internal links). Every signal contributes a non-positive
weight; trust_score = clamp(100 + Σ weights, 0, 100). 28 unit tests covering
fresh-site, stale-copyright, mixed-content, dead-links, and self-signed-cert
fixtures (httptest.Server + httptest.NewTLSServer). Per-fetch budget 5 s,
total cap 30 s, errgroup-bounded concurrency, SSRF guard via go-common
safehttp on every outbound URL. JSON envelope: url, trust_score, max_score,
breakdown (per-class), signals[], recommendations[], duration_ms.
